body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 19/2026
Made under: Health Professions And Occupations Act
Published in: No. 4 – B.C. Regs. 18 to 28, March 10, 2026
Deposit date: March 2, 2026

B.C. Reg. 19/2026, deposited March 2, 2026, under theHEALTH PROFESSIONS AND OCCUPATIONS ACT[section 645]. Order in Council 58/2026, approved and ordered March 2, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that paragraph (a) of B.C. Reg. 126/2025, is repealed and the following substituted: (a)	the following provisions of theHealth Professions and Occupations Act, S.B.C. 2022, c. 43, are brought into force: (i)	sections 3 to 8, 9 (1) (b) to (e) and (2), 10 to 13, 14 (1) and (4), 15 (3), 16 to 434, 440, 450 (2), 455 to 466, 467 (1), 468 to 484, 486 (1) (b), 488, 490 to 544 and 546 to 604; (ii)	section 605, except as it repeals and replaces the definition of"practitioner"in section 1 of thePharmacy Operations and Drug Scheduling Act; — J. OSBORNE,Minister of Health; C. BOYLE,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: Major health-profession regulation overhaul takes effect in BC
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has brought most sections of the Health Professions and Occupations Act into force. This law replaces the old Health Professions Act and creates a new system for regulating health professionals like doctors, nurses, and massage therapists. It sets up a single oversight body (the Health Professions and Occupations Board) and gives it new powers to set standards, investigate complaints, and discipline professionals. The change affects how health professionals are licensed, how they must report incidents, and how the public can file complaints.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Check if your profession is covered by the new Act and review updated licensing, reporting, and complaint requirements.

key_dates.label: Health Professions and Occupations Act new rules take effect in BC
